rulings.Case Title: KEITH DUNCAN VS. ALLAN SCHUMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Allan Schuman Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 1, Defendant Allan Schuman's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendant Allan Schuman's motion for summary judgment is granted. Mr. Schuman satisfied his initial summary judgment burden as to all three causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Keith Duncan by presenting admissible evidence that Mr. Duncan cannot prove any of those claims. The declarations of Jeffrey Greenberg and Mr. Schuman demonstrate that Mr. Schuman acted competently, did not breach a duty of care or his fiduciary duty to plaintiff and did not act with undue influence. Mr. Duncan failed to submit an opposing separate statement, points and authorities, or admissible evidence that creates a triable issue of material fact regarding any of his claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Schuman is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY PORTER VS. JML ENGINEERING & CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff Timothy Porter To Further Respond To Defendant Jml Engineering & Construction, Inc.'S Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $960.00 Against Plaintiff Timothy Porter And His Attorney Of Record, Mandissa Logan, Esq.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Gene Stone In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT JML ENGINEERING & CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff Timothy Porter To Further Respond To Defendant Jml Engineering & Construction, Inc.'S Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $960.00 Against Plaintiff Timothy Porter And His Attorney Of Record, Mandissa Logan, Esq. Judge Pro Tem William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wes@williamshapirolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW BAILEY VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel Psychological Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 10, Defendant'S Motion To Compel Psychological Independent Medical Examination Defendant Chinatown Community Development Center's motion to compel psychiatric and physical examination of plaintiff Andrew Bailey is granted in large part. Absent a stipulation to the contrary, the examination may not exceed five hours excluding breaks and must be completed in a single day. Mr. Bailey may take as many breaks as he feels he needs to take. While Mr. Bailey may not have his counsel or a support person in the room with him when he is being examined, he may have a support person of his choosing in a nearby location such as the examiner's waiting room and he may visit with that support person during the breaks of the examination. There will be no other restrictions or limitations on the examination. Mr. Bailey has not shown good cause for any other restrictions or limitations on the examination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCDC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ICON INC, A NEVADA CORPORATION VS. TADICH GRILL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTs TADICH GRILL, INC., and MICHAEL BUICH's Motion To Compel Arbitration Defendants Tadich Grill, Inc. and Michael Buich's motion to compel arbitration of the second and third causes of action for concealment and negligent misrepresentation in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Icon Inc. is denied to the extent that the second and third causes of action seek damages and other relief based on Icon's role as manager of the San Francisco restaurant and granted to the extent that the second and third causes of action seek damages and other relief not based on Icon's role as manager of the San Francisco restaurant. Since Icon contends that its second and third causes of action only seeks relief based on its role as manager of the San Francisco restaurant, Icon can proceed with those claims in this court, but in doing so Icon may not seek any relief that is not based on its role as manager of the San Francisco restaurant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Icon is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER ORAZIO VS. CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER ORAZIO's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint Plaintiff Jennifer Orazio's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted (except for the NIED claim against Cambria) and the trial is continued to June 24, 2019 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date. Ms. Orazio has until October 22, 2018 to file her first amended complaint. Granting leave to amend comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion is eliminated by the continuance of the trial. The court does not address defendants' arguments that the new claims are time-barred or otherwise deficient. Those challenges are best dealt with by a pleading motion such as a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Orazio is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: COLE RATIAS VS. RADIUS INTELLIGENCE, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 11, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT RADIUS INTELLIGENCE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Radius Intelligence, Inc.'s demurrer to all three causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Cole Ratias is overruled as to all three causes of action. Radius' request to take judicial notice of the 2009 separation agreement is denied because the parties dispute the validity and binding nature of that document. Mr. Ratias contends that the 2010 release superseded all prior contracts and Radius implicitly asserts that the 2009 separation agreement is binding. "[W]e hold the existence of a contract between private parties cannot be established by judicial notice under Evidence Code section 452, subdivision (h). Judicial notice under Evidence Code section 452, subdivision (h) is intended to cover facts which are not reasonably subject to dispute and are easily verified." (Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1145.) The judicial notice cases relied on by Radius are distinguishable because Mr. Ratias does not expressly refer to the 2009 agreement in the complaint. Mr. Ratias states a claim for breach of contract. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning which the contract is reasonably susceptible. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Radius fails to show that Mr. Ratias' ascribed meaning is unreasonable and the recitals are not irrelevant to the interpretation process. (Golden West Baseball Co. v. City of Anaheim (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 11, 37-38 (provision of lease was not "'mere' recital," and even if it was, resort to it was necessary to interpret covenants)). The face of the complaint does not necessarily show that any of the claims are barred by either the statute of limitations or laches. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Ratias is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
